question: He left the place in a hurry. (in a hurry)
explanation: It tells how the person left. This makes it an adverb phrase.

question: The house by the lake belongs to Chief Mapiri. (by the lake)
explanation: This phrase describes the house. It tells us which house it is.

question: Cleaning of the streets is appreciated by many. (Cleaning of the streets)
explanation: This group of words is the subject. It is the thing being appreciated.

question: The girl sitting on the bench is beautiful. (sitting on the bench)
options: C. noun phrase in apposition to ‘girl’
explanation: This phrase acts like an adjective. It tells us more about the girl.

question: The group decided to hold the meeting close to the office. (close to the office)
explanation: It tells where the meeting is held. This makes it an adverb phrase.

question: To find fault is easy. (To find fault)
explanation: This phrase is the subject of the sentence. It is the thing that is easy.

question: He speaks like a born orator. (like a born orator)
explanation: It describes the way he speaks. This means it is an adverb phrase.

question: Beyond a doubt, this man is honest. (Beyond a doubt)
explanation: This phrase describes how the man is honest. It modifies the verb.

question: He failed in spite of his best efforts. (in spite of his best efforts)
explanation: It gives a reason for the failure. It modifies the verb.

question: I have forgotten how to play this game. (how to play this game)
explanation: This phrase is the object. It is the thing that was forgotten.

question: The train is behind time. (behind time)
explanation: This phrase acts as an adverb. It tells us about the time.

question: He is a man of means. (of means)
explanation: It describes the man. It tells us what kind of man he is.

question: I want to go to the cinema today. (to go to the cinema today)
explanation: This phrase is the object. it is what the person wants.

question: He stood on the bridge at midnight. (on the bridge)
explanation: It tells where the person stood. This makes it an adverb phrase.

question: The cat slept under the tree. (under the tree)
explanation: This phrase tells us where the cat slept. It modifies the verb.

question: He treated his relatives with great kindness. (with great kindness)
explanation: It describes how he treated them. This is an adverb phrase.

question: The leader of the colony was William Bradford. (The leader of the colony)
explanation: The source says this is a complement. It helps complete the verb.

question: One of the early settlements disappeared. (One of the early settlements)
explanation: This phrase is the subject. It tells what disappeared.

question: The walk in space will be televised. (in space)
options: A. adverb phrase modifying ‘will be televised’
C. noun phrase subject of ‘will be televised’
explanation: This phrase describes the walk. It tells us which walk it is.

question: On the piano was a bowl of fresh flowers. (On the piano)
explanation: The source says this is the subject. It is the main part of the sentence.

question: He removed the tire with great ease. (with great ease)
explanation: This phrase tells how he removed the tire. It acts as an adverb.

question: After seeing the King, he departed. (After seeing the King)
explanation: It tells when the person departed. This makes it an adverb phrase.

question: He has gone to the river to bathe. (to bathe)
options: B. noun phrase object of ‘has gone’
explanation: This phrase tells why he went. It acts as an adverb.

question: To drive a car requires skill. (To drive a car)
explanation: This phrase is the subject. It is the thing that needs skill.

question: The postman looked very tired. (very tired)
explanation: This phrase describes the postman. It completes the linking verb.

question: I heard his answer spoken quietly. (spoken quietly)
explanation: The source says this is part of the object. It is what was heard.

question: The cackling of geese saved Rome. (The cackling of geese)
explanation: This phrase is the subject. It is what saved Rome.

question: A nod from a lord is breakfast for a fool. (A nod from a lord)
explanation: This phrase is the subject. It is what the sentence is about.

question: The farmer’s rosy children sat. (The farmer’s rosy children)
explanation: This phrase is the subject. It tells who sat down.

question: Home they brought her warrior dead. (her warrior dead)
explanation: This phrase is the object. It is what they brought home.

question: The crop bought from the young man was absolutely perfect. (bought from the young man)
explanation: It describes the crop. It tells us where the crop came from.

question: My sister and I carried the gear and studied the map. (studied the map)
options: C. verb phrase co-ordinate with ‘carried the gear’
explanation: This is a verb phrase. it is one of the actions done.

question: Lucky ran to the door and barked loudly. (barked loudly)
explanation: The source says this is an adverb phrase. It describes the action.

question: The message delivered in time was helpful. (delivered in time)
explanation: It describes the message. It tells us about the message.
